supply_chain.dependencies: Raw potatoes sourced primarily from Washington, Idaho, Oregon (US), Netherlands, Austria, Belgium, Germany, France, UK (Europe), Canada, China, Australia, Argentina. Significant portion sourced under strategic long-term grower relationships and short-term annual contracts. Edible oils sourced through strategic relationships with key suppliers. Packaging and energy inputs sourced through multiple suppliers.
supply_chain.risks: Potato crop performance dependent on environmental and climate conditions. Severe weather (extreme heat, cold, heavy rainfall, flooding) can significantly affect potato crop yield and quality. Potatoes susceptible to pests and diseases. Concentration of ideal growing conditions in few geographic regions globally. Unfavorable crop conditions in one region can lead to significant demand on other regions. Supply chain disruptions from execution issues, extreme weather, natural disasters, fire, terrorism, pandemics, armed hostilities, strikes, labor shortages, cybersecurity breaches, governmental restrictions, supplier capacity constraints. Increased costs for raw materials (potatoes, edible oils, packaging, grains, starches, energy). Limited number of competent high-quality co-packers. Third-party supplier, co-packer, logistics provider performance risks.

regulatory_risks: Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act compliance (FDA)
Food Safety Modernization Act compliance
Public Health Security and Bioterrorism Preparedness and Response Act compliance
Good Manufacturing Practices compliance
Nutrition Label Reform Act compliance
State and local food safety regulations
Product labeling and nutrition information requirements
Acrylamide regulation (FDA and EFSA guidance, California limits)
Environmental regulations (EPA and state agencies) - air, land, water discharge, waste handling, noise, odor emissions
Wastewater treatment requirements
Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) compliance
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance
Import/export restrictions, tariffs, sanctions, quotas, trade barriers
New or increased tariffs (including April 2025 US tariffs on imports)
Retaliatory tariffs by foreign governments
Trade agreement modifications or terminations
Currency exchange controls and capital controls
Climate change regulations (greenhouse gas reduction, water rights)
Packaging material regulations and bans
Front of pack labeling requirements
Allergen labeling requirements
False or deceptive advertising claims (federal and state consumer protection laws)
Product recall and withdrawal requirements
Antitrust law compliance
Immigration law changes affecting labor recruitment

sanction_risks: Trade protection measures by foreign governments favoring local producers
